No artistic experience is needed—just a spirit of curiosity and a willingness to play.

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms
description: Qigong & the Poetry of Psalms Following the success of a fall class, guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman returns to the Farm for a joyful, cross-cultural exploration of Qigong & the Poetry of Tehillim— Psalms in Hebrew. With its gentle movements and guided breathing, Qigong will help us to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies.

title: Havdalah on the Farm
description: Havdalah on the Farm Havdalah, literally meaning separation, is a ritual held at the end of Shabbat, after the sun has set and when three stars appear. There are blessings over wine, a candle, and spices. It is a ritual that allows us to hold onto the sweet moments of Shabbat for just a few more minutes before going into our busy week. Join Coastal Roots Farm and The Hive on Saturday, February 19th from 7 - 8:30 PM on the Farm for a special Havdalah service led by Sharone Oren and Ashly Tamayo.

title: Winter Gardening Workshop
description: Join us for a special Winter Gardening Workshop: Tree Pruning. Learn with Christina Cranwell, our experienced Outdoor Educator who has been farming 5+ years across multiple states. There is no experience necessary to join, gloves and materials provided. Cover all the basics of tree maintenance to promote healthy tree form, fruiting, and learn how to choose the right pruning tool to protect the longevity of your fruit tree.

title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
location: California Watersports at Agua Hedionda Lagoon, Carlsbad, CA
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline.

title: Birding & Brews

description: Be an Ocean Connector and start your day with birding and brews! Then, head out on a guided walking tour along the Bayshore Bikeway at South San Diego Bay, equipped with binoculars and field guides. What to Expect: Coffee Tasting: Kick off the tour with a unique nitro cold brew tasting, crafted locally by Trident Coffee.

title: Compost and Mulch Giveaway – 5/30 at Chollas Lake, Gloria’s Mesa Park
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours.

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special.

title: Community Restoration Day
location: Living Coast Refuge, Chula Vista, CA
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us in restoring San Diego's vital wetland habitats! Get hands-on experience in restoring coastal sagescrub habitats by helping remove invasive species, planting native plants, and supporting local wildlife. Each event wraps up with a guided nature walk where you can spot native birds and learn more about the area’s natural ecosystem. What to Expect: Invasive Species Removal : Help clear invasive plants that threaten local biodiversity.

title: Plant. Prepare. Pray: Shavuot Learning and Celebration
description: Shavuot, the Jewish holiday that commemorates the giving of the Torah on Mount Sinai, is also a deeply agricultural celebration. In ancient Israel, it marked the time when farmers brought bikkurim—the first fruits of their harvest—to Jerusalem as a gesture of gratitude and connection. After offering their choicest fruits and engaging in sacred reconnection, they returned home to enjoy the bounty of their labor.

title: Monthly Farm Tour
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
description: Savor the Harvest at the June Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Harvest Kitchen—a full-service catering company based in North County. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings.

That’s our hope for this Fest.

title: Paint & Nosh
description: Join us for an inspiring evening of art, community, and connection at Paint and Nosh! Set against the scenic backdrop of the Farm, this event invites you to explore your creativity while enjoying delicious refreshments. Guided by guest artist Tracy Lewin and our knowledgeable Farm Educators, you’ll create your own painting while learning about peleh—the Jewish value of wonder. Tracy Lewin is a local San Diego artist who is passionate about bringing art to the masses.

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the County of San Diego for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. description: About the Series Each of us can grow and deepen the ways we relate to our earth. Let's explore this through earth-based Judaism! This five-part journey offers a chance to explore powerful Jewish emotional-spiritual frameworks that deepen our relationship with the natural world.

title: Great Outdoor Shabbat
description: Coastal Roots Farm invites people of all ages and backgrounds to our free, family friendly, and festive Summer Shabbat series—Great Outdoor Shabbat! It's a true picnic-style Shabbat: bring a picnic dinner, drinks, and blankets or chairs. We'll provide live music, a fun craft for kids, a brief 10–15 minute tour of the Farm, playtime in Nature Play, and optional learning opportunities. As the sun sets over the fields, we’ll welcome Shabbat together in the beauty of the great outdoors.

description: Mandala is Sanskrit for “circle” and is most commonly associated with Hindu and Buddhist cultures. Join the Farm’s Director of Jewish Life, Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a hands-on, Jewish take on outdoor mandala-making, in commemoration of the upcoming Jewish fast day of Tisha B'Av. Make your own unique mandala using organic materials from the Farm’s Food Forest and engage in a conversation about the many ways we can love -- freely and abundantly.

title: Tishrei Ag Fest
description: The cycle of agriculture requires that we continually regenerate, repair, and renew in order to grow. Likewise, the Hebrew month of Tishrei, often referred to as the “month of beginning,” is the first month of the Jewish year and reminds us that such renewal is possible at the human level too: with each new year, we reflect where we have been and where we’re going so that we may begin again with intention.

title: 42nd Annual Coastal Cleanup Day

description: The Ocean Conservancy and the California Coastal Commission partner with volunteer organizations, such as I Love A Clean San Diego to create the largest, international cleanup day. description: Join us this summer for a 5-part series featuring inspiring documentary films, local musicians, and meaningful conversations around equitable food systems, environmental justice, and caring for the planet. description: Join us for an evening of cooking, connection, and farm-fresh flavors. Together, we’ll prepare a Farm-Fresh menu using ingredients harvested straight from the Farm. Through this hands-on experience, you’ll have the chance to harvest fresh produce, learn new culinary skills, and share a nourishing meal with fellow participants.

description: Join us for the 10th Annual Sukkot Harvest Festival at Coastal Roots Farm! The Sukkot Harvest Festival is the Farm's biggest event of the year, inspired by the Jewish tradition of gathering community outdoors to celebrate the end of an abundant summer harvest and welcome in the fall season and all it brings. Meet us under the sukkah (temporary shelter) for a day filled with live music, food, and activities for all ages.

description: Sukkot Shabbat Dinner Join Director of Jewish Life Rebecca Joy Fletcher for a celebration of community, soil, and soul in this Shabbat and Sukkot dinner celebration inside the Farm sukkah! Connect with friends new and old, share stories of abundance and harvest, and engage in Sukkot learning and ritual which bind us more closely to love of the earth and its bounty. There will be opportunity to meet, laugh, and sing! No need to identify as Jewish to thoroughly enjoy.

description: Festival Activities Sponsors Tickets When it comes to celebrating the earth, ancient Jewish tradition recommends we do it together. Coastal Roots Farm invites you to celebrate with us at our 13th annual Sukkot Harvest Festival, our biggest event of the year! This intergenerational event features live music, local food, hands-on workshops, and engaging activities for all ages as we celebrate the bounty of our Farm and the spirit of our diverse community.

title: Pride in the Sukkah
description: LGBTQ+ Sukkot Celebration at Coastal Roots Farm Come celebrate the beauty of LGBTQ+ community and the harvest holiday of Sukkot under Coastal Roots Farm’s big sukkah! Together we’ll gather with friends old and new, share festive food and drink, and explore the meaning of Sukkot through Earth Based Judaism learning. Sukkot is one of the most communal Jewish holidays. In the sukkah, a traditional temporary outdoor structure, we leave the walls open so that everyone feels welcome.

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & Psalms
description: Qigong & Psalms Join Coastal Roots Farm and guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman, Director of Spiritual Life at Jewish Family Service, for an embodied exploration of Qigong & Psalms. In this four-part, interactive series, we will draw on qigong to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies while connecting more deeply to the imagery and inspiration found in the ancient Book of Psalms.

title: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle
description: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. What is Dayenu?Dayenu is a national Jewish movement responding to the climate crisis with hope, action, and community. Local Dayenu Circles bring people together to learn, connect, and take meaningful steps toward a more just and sustainable future. You don’t need to identify as Jewish to participate—everyone is welcome.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner
description: Savor the Harvest at the October Farm-to-Table Dinner Celebrate the bounty of our 18-acre regenerative farm with an exquisite, seasonally inspired dinner crafted by Chef Alex Velasco. Enjoy an intimate evening of community connection in the heart of the Farm, featuring a curated multi-course pescatarian menu filled with organic vegetables, sustainably sourced fish, and thoughtful wine and beer pairings. With a 60-guest maximum, this experience ensures a cozy and communal atmosphere.

title: The Big Birthday Block Party
description: Enjoy free admission to the Museum, explore all exhibits, a 21+ rooftop bar, enjoy food, drink, music, deals, and more.

title: Sukkot Harvest Festival 2024
description: When it comes to celebrating the earth, ancient Jewish tradition recommends we do it together. Coastal Roots Farm invites you to celebrate with us at our 12th annual Sukkot Harvest Festival, our biggest event of the year! This intergenerational event features live music, local food, hands-on workshops, and engaging activities for all ages as we celebrate the bounty of our Farm and the spirit of our diverse community.

title: SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering
description: The SoCal Tribal Food Sovereignty Gathering at Coastal Roots Farm is an opportunity for Tribal members in Southern California that are actively cultivating or interested in joining the food sovereignty movement among Indigenous Nations. Examples of people active in food sovereignty work include Tribal farmers, seed keepers, land stewards, hunters, livestock owners, foragers, botanists, fishers, medicine makers, and cooks.

description: Dine with us during an intimate Farm-to-Table Dinner at Coastal Roots Farm! Featuring Chef Francesco Bonsi Sicilian-born, locally-based personal chef and food educator rooted in supporting local farms and giving guests a true taste of Italy in each meal that he creates. Including seasonal, organic farm-fresh produce. Supporting the Farm's impact in the community.

title: Intro to Earth-Based Judaism Series (4 Classes)
description: In this four-part pilot class, we will gather in lovely locations across the Farm to explore 4 basic pathways into the spiritual revolution called Earth-Based Judaism -- and how to bring those pathways back into your life. The goal is more resilience, earth-love, responsibility, and joy. Our learning will include sacred Jewish texts and wisdom, hearty conversation, on-our-feet exploration and play, and moments of quiet reflection. We'll also provide coffee, pastries, and time to schmooze.

You will learn the elements fundamental to the practice-both the how and the why - and have a great time doing it! All Yoga promotes stress relief, relaxation, overall health, and well-being.

title: Farm Film and Music Series
description: Experience Film, Music, and Meaningful Conversation on the Farm Join us on the Farm for an evening of community, live music, and transformative storytelling. Part of our 5-part Farm Film & Music Series, this event invites guests to gather under the stars and engage in inspiring dialogue about regenerative agriculture, environmental justice, and the future of our food systems. We're screening two documentaries: Faka'apa'apa and an early draft release of Out of Mined (coming March 2026).

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
location: Lamar County Park, Spring Valley, CA
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego and the County of San Diego for a litter cleanup! We will divert litter from our watersheds and keep it from traveling to the Pacific Ocean. Our staff will have check-in ready for you on-site. Be sure to wear comfortable clothes, closed-toed shoes, some form of sun protection (sunscreen or a hat), and bring a water bottle or two! Staff will be providing everything else you will need! Register below for more information and to get signed up!

description: Offered the last Sunday of every month at 11:30 AM. Vermicomposting uses worms to convert waste into fertilizer for your garden! Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

title: Compost Workshop
description: Compost Workshops Offered every Sunday at 11:30 AM. Composting turns organic waste that would end up in a landfill into nutrient-rich soil. Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

title: Earth Based Judaism: Qigong & Psalms
description: Qigong & Psalms Join Coastal Roots Farm and guest teacher Rabbi Susan Freeman, Director of Spiritual Life at Jewish Family Service, for an embodied exploration of Qigong & Psalms. In this four-part, interactive series, we will draw on qigong to experience the elements of water, wood, fire, earth, and metal in our bodies while connecting more deeply to the imagery and inspiration found in the ancient Book of Psalms.

description: Join us in celebrating Havdalah on the Farm and mark the end of Shabbat! Spend time in the great outdoors and gather in community to usher in the new week. Activities include: making a farm-fresh besamim (spice bag) and tasting local wines. Plus! A chance to make your own locally-sourced beeswax candle. Havdalah ceremony will be led by Camp Mountain Chai's musical specialist, Marshall Voit followed by dancing and music. Family friendly. Pre-registration encouraged. Register now

description: Monthly Dayenu Advocacy Circle Join us at Coastal Roots Farm for our new Dayenu Circle, a monthly advocacy group rooted in Jewish values and hope-filled climate action. Local Dayenu Circles bring people together to learn, connect, and take meaningful steps toward a more just and sustainable future. You don’t need to identify as Jewish to participate—everyone is welcome.

Enjoy root veggie latkes (with Farm veggies) made fresh by The Hive's Chef Tiffani, along with apple cider to keep you warm. There will be ample heat lamp and blankets.

Lower your carbon footprint, divert waste, and enhance your garden all with one solution. Join us for a one-hour workshop to get you started and answer questions. (Included with cost of admission) On the first Sunday of the month, the compost workshop will be offered in English and Spanish.

title: Volunteer to Support Us at Our Office!
organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
description: Have you wondered how you could get more involved? We are growing and need your help so we can all do more good together! We are looking for amazing volunteers to support our field prep and office work. We can verify community service hours (including court-ordered hours) for your time at these events.

description: Offered the last Sunday of every month at 11:30 AM. Vermicomposting uses worms to convert waste into fertilizer for your garden! This course will teach you how you can get started with your own worm bin and how to care for your worm friends! This class is included with admission. Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

title: Let's Speak Spanish
description: This is a Spanish conversation group for intermediate and proficient speakers. We will meet and discuss various topics in Spanish. The group meets the 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month.
